title: Youth Employment Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Employment > Youth Employment Programs 37 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter African American Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness ANTFARM YOUTHCORE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a hands-on training program teaching basic employment skills such as landscaping, debris removal, land cleaing, invavis species and brush removal, junk hauling and more. All program participants become employees of the agency. 39140 Proctor Boulevard Sandy, OR 97055 Eligibility: Ages 14-24 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (503) 668-7962 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BESTCARE TREATMENT SERVICES MADRAS MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES YOUNG ADULTS IN TRANSITION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support services for young adults that include transitional treatment and intervention to help them graduate from school, continue their education, find employment, maintain friendships, and gain independence. 850 SW 4th Street Suite 302 Madras, OR 97741 Eligibility: Ages 14-24 who are living with mental health and/or behavioral challenges Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 475-6575 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BESTCARE TREATMENT SERVICES PRINEVILLE MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES YOUNG ADULTS IN TRANSITION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support services for young adults that include transitional treatment and intervention to help them graduate from school, continue their education, find employment, maintain friendships, and gain independence. 1059 NW Madras Highway Prineville, OR 97754 Eligibility: Ages 14-24 who are living with mental health and/or behavioral challenges Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 323-5330 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOYS & GIRLS CLUB OF CORVALLIS BOYS & GIRLS CLUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a positive space for youth to engage in fun and empowering programs, such as leadership and community service, after-school programs, academic enrichment, workforce training, performing and visual arts, camps, and personal health and fitness. 1112 NW Circle Boulevard Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Ages 5-18 Hours: Hours vary by activity. Regular after-school programming Monday-Friday after school until 6pm. Email (541) 757-1909 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOYS & GIRLS CLUB OF EMERALD VALLEY BOYS & GIRLS CLUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a positive space for youth to engage in fun and empowering programs, such as leadership and community service, after-school programs, academic enrichment, workforce training, performing and visual arts, camps, and personal health and fitness. 1545 W 22nd Avenue Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Ages 6-18 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5:30pm Email (541) 345-9939 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER THE LOFT YOUTH EMPLOYMENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers resume workshops, mock interviews, strengths assessments, goal setting, career exploration, and volunteer opportunities. Those enrolled in the program can earn gift cards, access bus passes, and receive interview clothes. Eligibility: Ages 14-24 without housing, family, or community connections Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (800) 660-0934 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHEHALEM YOUTH & FAMILY SERVICES YOOP! MCMINNVILLE SKILL CENTER YOUTH OPPORTUNITY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational and vocational opportunities for youth and young adults. Services include pre-employment training, Rethinking Careers Workshops, GED preparation classes, college application assistance, student financial aid assistance. Offers assistance in obtaining documentation such as ID card, Social Security card, and birth certificate. Parenting classes available for youth who have children. Child care is not available at skill centers, but vouchers may be available. 117 NE 5th Street Suite H McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Ages 16-24. Must meet income guidelines. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm by appointment Email (503) 883-9768 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHEHALEM YOUTH & FAMILY SERVICES YOOP! NEWBERG SKILL CENTER YOUTH OPPORTUNITY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational and vocational opportunities for youth and young adults. Services include pre-employment training, Rethinking Careers Workshops, GED preparation classes, college application assistance, student financial aid assistance. Offers assistance in obtaining documentation such as ID card, Social Security card, and birth certificate. Parenting classes available for youth who have children. Child care is not available at skill centers, but vouchers may be available. 100 E 5th Street Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Ages 16-24. Must meet income guidelines. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (503) 554-1461 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT C-TEC YOUTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance to the emerging workforce to assist in preparing to be successful in education and employment. Services include assistance with career planning, training, work experience, National Career Readiness Certificate, and job finding. 13455 SE 97th Avenue Clackamas, OR 97015 Eligibility: Ages 14-24 who are eligible to work in the United States and meet low-income requirements Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (located on the Clackamas Community College campus) Email (503) 594-3964 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY SERVICES CONSORTIUM ALBANY YOUTH HOUSE YOUTH HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides GED test preperation and assistance with GED test fees. Also provides a paid work experiance opptunity. Work experiance pays minimum wage (examples of work experiance include community garden, MakerSpace, city library, maintenance or local business) 130 SE 4th Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Varies by program with a focus on youth ages 16-24. Hours: No standard hours (541) 758-2631 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY SERVICES CONSORTIUM ALBANY YOUTH HOUSE YOUTH BUILD Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a youth employment program with a focus on assisting community partners with a variety of construction, land restoration, and essential maintenance projects. Also helps participants work toward their GED or high school diplomas. 130 SE 4th Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Ages 18-24 Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm (541) 758-2631 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY SERVICES CONSORTIUM CORVALLIS YOUTH HOUSE AND GARDENS YOUTH HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides GED test preperation and assistance with GED test fees. Also provides a paid work experiance opptunity. Work experiance pays minimum wage (examples of work experiance include community garden, MakerSpace, city library, maintenance or local business) 536 2nd Street SW Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Varies by program with a focus on youth ages 16-24. Hours: No standard hours (541) 758-2605 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY SERVICES CONSORTIUM HENRY HILL POLK COUNTY YOUTH PROGRAM POLK GED AND CAREER PATHWAYS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides GED test preperation and assistance with GED test fees. Also provides a paid work experiance opptunity. Work experiance pays minimum wage and is a total of 200 hours (examples of work experiance include community garden, MakerSpace, city library, maintenance or local business) 750 5th Street S Independence, OR 97351 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 and not enrolled in highschool or college Hours: Phone Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 704-7820 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONNECTED LANE COUNTY SPARK AT BOOTH KELLY ELEVATE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers career exploration and hands-on learning. Organizes hiring fairs, career expos, job shadows, and internships based on young people's interests, while giving them the tools and training they need to succeed at work. 303 S 5th Street Suite 150 Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Ages 14-24 Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 654-4095 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONNECTED LANE COUNTY SPARK AT BOOTH KELLY EXCELERATORS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides accelerated education, training, certifications, and hands-on paid internships with a focus on culinary, technology, healthcare, and manufacturing jobs. Transportation assistance, required PPE (personal protective equipment), other work-related gear, and lunch are also provided. 303 S 5th Street Suite 150 Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: All programs: Ages 18-24 Healthcare program: Must pass a drug test, background check, and have up-to-date vaccinations (hepatitis B, varicella, MMR, flu), and must meet mandatory attendance requirements Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 654-4095 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONNECTED LANE COUNTY SPARK ON 7TH ELEVATE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers career exploration and hands-on learning. Organizes hiring fairs, career expos, job shadows, and internships based on young people's interests, while giving them the tools and training they need to succeed at work. 22 W 7th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Ages 14-24 Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 654-4095 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONNECTED LANE COUNTY SPARK ON 7TH EXCELERATORS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides accelerated education, training, certifications, and hands-on paid internships with a focus on culinary, technology, healthcare, and manufacturing jobs. Transportation assistance, required PPE (personal protective equipment), other work-related gear, and lunch are also provided. 22 W 7th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: All programs: Ages 18-24 Healthcare program: Must pass a drug test, background check, and have up-to-date vaccinations (hepatitis B, varicella, MMR, flu), and must meet mandatory attendance requirements Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 654-4095 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COVE CHRISTIAN CAMP SUMMER CAMPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers summer camps. 68405 Mill Creek Lane Cove, OR 97824 Eligibility: Grades 1-12 Hours: Tuesday, June 16, 2026-Saturday, July 18, 2026 Email (541) 568-4662 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DESCHUTES COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH YOUNG ADULTS IN TRANSITION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Young Adults in Transition (YAT) provides support services for young adults who suffer from mental health or behavioral challenges. YAT Teams establish relationships with young adults, families and peers, and all available support systems. Transitional treatment and intervention help young adults graduate school, continue education, find employment, maintain friendships, and gain independence. 2577 NE Courtney Drive Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Ages 14-24 who are involved with the juvenile justice system, Department of Human Services currently homeless, or live in a foster home. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 213-6851 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ELKTON COMMUNITY EDUCATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides cultural and educational services including nature and art experiences as well as classes and events. 15850 Highway 38 Elkton, OR 97436 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Office and Gift Shop: Varies by season Library: Tuesday/Thursday 10am-2pm and second Saturday of the month 10am-2pm Email (541) 584-2692 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ELKTON COMMUNITY EDUCATION CENTER YOUTH EMPLOYMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers employment experience to youth in catering, gardening, retail, customer service, butterfly raising and research, and historical tour guiding. 15850 Highway 38 Elkton, OR 97436 Eligibility: Ages 14-18 Hours: Varies Email (541) 584-2692 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HAND IN HAND FARM INC OF OREGON LIFE SKILLS TRAINING AND MENTORING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers family skills training, vocational training, and parenting education through agricultural and equine interactions. Online parenting classes provided. Individuals must wear recommended clothing and shoes to participate in activities. In-person services for family education should involve at least one parent/guardian and their dependent. 35105 Ede Road Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Unrestricted, with a focus on individuals with autism spectrum disorder. Rebuilding Your Self and Your Family Zoom class is for parents or guardians only. Hours: Varies by service. In-person services must be scheduled in advance. Email (541) 451-1243 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEART OF OREGON CORPS YEAR-ROUND PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers four, year-round student programs where members have the opportunity to earn wages and high school "skills-based" credits during their time of service. Crews work on a variety of environmental projects throughout the year and gain job skills. Programs include: High Desert Conservation Corps, Stewardship, YouthBuild, and Food Heros. 1291 NE 5th Street Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Ages 16-24. Specific age and eligibility varies by program. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 633-7834 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEART OF OREGON CORPS CENTRAL OREGON YOUTH CONSERVATION CORPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Hires and employs youth to work on public lands in a natural resources-based, hands-on education and employment program. Participants do hard physical work and may be exposed to insects, adverse weather, and difficult working conditions. Participants earn minimum wage and can earn an industrial certification. Transportation from Heart of Oregon Corps to the work site is provided. 1291 NE 5th Street Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Youth ages 16-18 at the date of enrollment. Must be permanent residents of the U.S. and eligible for employment. No specific experience is required. Hours: Program employment: June-August, 4 days per week Email (541) 633-7834 . Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEART OF OREGON CORPS YOUTHBUILD Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Work-earn-learn program that gives young people construction skills training along with help toward their GED or diploma while earning a stipend. The young people also help construct affordable housing or support child and youth development within their community. 1291 NE 5th Street Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Ages 16-24. Emphasis is placed on recruiting "opportunity youth" who are not in school or employed. Hours: Monday-Friday 7am-4:30pm Email (541) 526-1380 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEART OF OREGON CORPS FOOD HEROS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers youth employment program to learn skills and contribute to the community by growing fresh produce to provide to community organizations like the Madras Food Pantry and Meals on Wheels. Program offers hands-on experience, certifications in supply chain, warehousing, logistics, and food handling. 1291 NE 5th Street Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Ages 17-24 "opportunity youth" without school or work Hours: Daily 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 633-7834 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEART OF OREGON CORPS GO! LEAD Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers employment opportunities, job training, and environmental education for youth interested in Camp LEAD or have attended in the past. Participants are paid to help maintain public recreation areas and learn about outdoor related careers. Employment takes place at Camp LEAD, and participants have opportunities to engage in activities, play games, hone camp skills, and make friends. 1291 NE 5th Street Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 experiencing a disability Hours: Varies Email (541) 633-7834 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOSEA YOUTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides meals, clothing, laundry, personal hygiene items, showers, job training classes, mentoring programs, referrals to other resources, and access to telephone and computer services. For the winter season, offering tents, sleeping bags, and blankets. 834 Monroe Street Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 with a focus on individuals experiencing or at risk of homelessness Hours: Monday-Thursday 11am-6:30pm, Friday 11am-3pm Lunch: noon-2pm Dinner: 5pm Email (541) 344-5583 - Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMPACT NW IMPACT NW DANCING TREE RESOURCE CENTER CORE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A 3-month curriculum focused on assisting youth in developing job skills such as resume writing, creating employment opportunities through partnerships with local businesses, and college and career exploration. 10055 E Burnside Street Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Grades 9-12 Hours: Hours vary Email (503) 721-6751 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ISAAC'S ROOM IKE QUEST Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Focuses on life skills, work training and experience, internships, mentoring, and enrichment by expanding horizons. Offers a place for youth to make connections and explore opportunities in the community. 299 Cottage Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Ages 16-21 Hours: Varies Email (503) 581-6154 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES RURAL PROGRAM LOOKING GLASS RURAL PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a drop-in center with access to food, clothing, and other vital resources. Provides case management services to assist youth in finding and maintaining stable housing. Also helps with employment including resume preparation, job skills training, and connections to employment opportunities. 620 E Whiteaker Avenue Cottage Grove, OR 97424 Eligibility: Youth ages 11-21 who are homeless, runaways, or at risk of becoming homeless Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 767-3823 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MANO A MANO FAMILY CENTER COLONIA AMISTAD POSITIVE YOUTH DEVELOPMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides youth with mentoring, tutoring, and case management to help them graduate, find stable employment, and housing. Offers after-school programs, volunteer opportunities, and leadership development. 30 S Gun Club Road Independence, OR 97351 Eligibility: Unrestricted Strengthening Families: Ages 10-14 and their parents Hours: Monday-Thursday noon-6pm Email (503) 315-2290 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MANO A MANO FAMILY CENTER COLONIA LIBERTAD POSITIVE YOUTH DEVELOPMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides youth with mentoring, tutoring, and case management to help them graduate, find stable employment, and housing. Offers after-school programs, volunteer opportunities, and leadership development. 2921 Saddle Club Street SE #1009 Salem, OR 97317 Eligibility: Unrestricted Strengthening Families: Ages 10-14 and their parents Hours: Monday-Thursday noon-6pm Email (503) 315-2290 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MANO A MANO FAMILY CENTER NORTHGATE CENTER POSITIVE YOUTH DEVELOPMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides youth with mentoring, tutoring, and case management to help them graduate, find stable employment, and housing. Offers after-school programs, volunteer opportunities, and leadership development. 3850 Portland Road NE Suite 130 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Strengthening Families: Ages 10-14 and their parents Hours: Monday-Thursday noon-6pm Email (503) 363-1895 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY COMMUNITY ACTION HOME YOUTH SERVICES HOME YOUTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a day shelter with drop-in services and an overnight emergency shelter. Supports youth with meals, laundry, showers, personal items, and community connection. Accepts school supply donations. Programs include: Runaway and Homeless Youth Program (RHY): Connects youth and their families to resources that meet their immediate needs. Also provides mediation and counseling resources for family reunification. The goal is safe exits from the streets, achieved through relationships with local law enforcement, street outreach, and working closely with the school district and other community partners. Youth Empowerment Program (YEP): Offered in 4 elements including internships and jobs, financial literacy, workshops and field trips, and extended support. Youth cohorts go through the program in 3-month cycles and can earn high school credit for their work and experiences during the program. Taylor's House is a 10 bed emergency shelter thay offers services for study support, social activities, job training. Onsite treatment and counseling services, as well as meeting rooms for appointments, visits and recreation. 625 Union Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Ages 11-17, 18 if still in school or working towards a GED Hours: The Drop In: Noon-7pm / 7 days per week Office: Monday-Friday 8am-6:30pm Email (971) 273-7300 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH BEN & JERRY’S PARTNERSHOP PROMOTING AVENUES TO EMPLOYMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers job training, paid work experiences, and support for finding jobs and careers. 542 SW Yamhill Street Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Age 16-24 who are who are at-risk or experiencing housing instability Hours: 7 days per week noon-7pm Email (503) 267-4382 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH NEW DAY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supports the safety, needs, and rights of people experiencing sex trafficking or exploitation, trading sex, or who are at risk. Provides case outreach, case management/advocacy, and counseling. Helps with safety planning, transportation, and access to shelter or other housing resources. Also offers referral to additional resources, access to employment and education programs, and LGBTQ2+ resources. 314 SW 9th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 12-25 who have been impacted by sex trafficking Hours: Referral Line: Monday-Friday 10am-6pm Hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 258-7081 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH PROMOTING AVENUES TO EMPLOYMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers job training, paid work experiences, and support for finding jobs and careers. 314 SW 9th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Age 16-24 who are who are at-risk or experiencing housing instability Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 267-4382 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH NEW AVENUES INK PROMOTING AVENUES TO EMPLOYMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers job training, paid work experiences, and support for finding jobs and careers. 1657 SE 6th Avenue Suite 6 Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Age 16-24 who are who are at-risk or experiencing housing instability Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 267-4382 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTSIDE IN OUTSIDE IN EMPLOYMENT RESOURCE CENTER EDUCATION AND EMPLOYMENT RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers GED preparation and testing, GED/high school credit recovery and tutoring that is Portland Public School accredited, work readiness training, job search help, resume help, college preparation, and help with post-secondary education and training. "Urban Ed" is a drop-in, student-centered alternative school for youth who have dropped out or are at risk of dropping out of school. Work experiences are available at the Virginia Woof (VW) Dog Daycare and Youth Job Training Program. Child care is also available for established clients. 1132 SW 13th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 18-24 who are low-income, homeless, or at risk of homelessness Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday 10am-1pm and 2pm-5pm, Wednesday/Friday 10am-1pm Email (503) 445-0976 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND YOUTHBUILDERS YOUTHBUILD PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers an alternative high school and job training program for young people who are ready to earn a GED or high school diploma and launch a career. Provides education, skills, structure, and GED test instruction. Program includes academic instruction in small classes leading to a GED or diploma; hands-on job training in technology or construction; industry-recognized certifications, preparation for college, apprenticeship, and career-path employment, career coaching, and wrap-around support; free bus passes; opportunities to earn an AmeriCorps education award and small financial bonuses for achievement; and multi-year support and placement assistance after graduation. Students attend school full-time for roughly 12 months and split their time between the classroom and PYB jobsites. In PYB's Technology track, students learn coding and programming and build and upgrade computers as a community service. PYB's Construction track is a certified pre-apprenticeship program in which students build and renovate homes for low-income families. PYB helps students in both tracks obtain employment and post-secondary education or training. 4816 SE 92nd Avenue Portland, OR 97266 Eligibility: Low-income individuals ages 17-24. Applicants ages 17-20 must live within a partnering school district. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (503) 286-9350 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SATURDAY ACADEMY EDUCATIONAL STEAM PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of hands-on STEAM focused classes and camps for grades K-12. Internship programs include Apprenticeships in Science and Engineering (ASE) for high school students. 4035 NE Sandy Boulevard Suite 226 Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Grades K-12 Hours: Varies by class Email (503) 200-5858 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UPLIFT LEADERSHIP EARLY ENTREPRENEURS INITIATIVE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a year-round education program that helps participants create and build their own businesses, products, services, or brands. Also provides a community calendar of events that focus on job readiness and career development. 525 3rd Street Suite 200 Lake Oswego, OR 97034 Eligibility: Ages 16-26 Hours: No standard hours Email (971) 266-1266 Get Directions Visit Website More Details URBAN LEAGUE OF PORTLAND YOUTH PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers youth programs with interactive academic and real-world experiences that build leadership, life, and social skills, self-esteem, and cultural pride. Programs are designed to prepare students for success, encouraging post-secondary education and professional careers. Services include scholarship listings, job readiness training, employment support, and internship opportunities. 3934 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Ages 12-25. Focuses on serving the African American community and additional communities of color. Hours: Hours vary Email (503) 280-2600 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COMMUNITY ACTION PARTNERSHIP YOUTH OUTREACH SERVICES JOBS READINESS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides job readiness and development support for youth and young adults. Provides resume help, job workshops, follow-up assistance, skill building, interview practice, career assessment, and educational support. 719 E 1st Street Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Ages 14-24 Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-8pm (503) 538-8023 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COMMUNITY ACTION PARTNERSHIP YOUTH OUTREACH SERVICES YOUTH OUTREACH DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & hygiene supplies, and information and referral relating to in-house shelter services. 719 E 1st Street Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Ages 11-24 Hours: Drop in: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-noon
